mcq.question: What is a sensor?
mcq.correct: A device that measures a physical property and converts it into an electrical signal

mcq.question: What type of data do most sensors produce?
What is used to convert sensor data into digital form?
Which sensor is used in car parking systems?
A greenhouse system needs to monitor temperature automatically. Which sensor should be used?
Why is an ADC needed with sensors?
mcq.correct: Because computers can only process digital data

mcq.question: What happens after a sensor collects data in an automated system?
mcq.correct: The signal is converted to digital by an ADC and sent to the processor
wrong: The data is stored permanently immediately
The actuator is activated straight away
The data is displayed to the user

mcq.question: What does the processor do with data from a sensor?
mcq.correct: Compares it to stored or set values and decides what action to take
wrong: Sends it directly to an output device
Deletes the data after reading it

mcq.question: What is an output in an automated system?
mcq.correct: An action carried out by an actuator or output device
wrong: The data collected by the sensor
The signal sent to the ADC

mcq.question: A temperature sensor sends data to a system controlling heating. What happens next?
mcq.correct: The processor compares the value to a set temperature and decides whether to turn heating on
wrong: The data is automatically displayed and ignored
The sensor directly switches the heating on
The data is stored without processing
